req.title: Read patient (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care; reading outside their assignment shall be denied. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.
preconditions: role:clinician

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-027 defines three key test dimensions: (1) happy-path access where a clinician reads a patient in their care, (2) denial when the clinician is not assigned to that patient, and (3) boundary values on FHIR Patient fields such as birthDate (epoch minimum/maximum) and numeric identifiers at edge values. EP covers the valid assignment partition; NEG covers the forbidden partition; BVA covers the timestamp and numeric element edges. A DT case is added to cover the combined decision of role x assignment to ensure the auth logic is complete. An STC case covers the audit-write state transition confirming the audit log entry is created after a successful read.

tests.name: test_clinician_reads_assigned_patient_returns_200
preconditions: Clinician user exists with role=clinician
Patient resource exists and is assigned to the clinician
Valid bearer token obtained for the clinician
expected: Response body id matches the requested patient id
trace: R-HLT-027 positive path: clinician reads patient in their care
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_clinician_reads_assigned_patient_returns_200(
    api_client,
    clinician_token,
    assigned_patient_id,
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{assigned_patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Patient"
    assert body["id"] == str(assigned_patient_id)

tests.name: test_clinician_reads_unassigned_patient_returns_403
preconditions: Clinician user exists with role=clinician
Patient resource exists but is NOT assigned to the clinician
Valid bearer token obtained for the clinician
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error message indicating access denied
trace: R-HLT-027 error pathway: clinician not assigned to patient
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_clinician_reads_unassigned_patient_returns_403(
    api_client,
    clinician_token,
    unassigned_patient_id,
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{unassigned_patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    issue_text = str(body)
    assert any(
        kw in issue_text.lower()
        for kw in ["forbidden", "access denied", "not authorized", "operationoutcome"]
    )

tests.name: test_patient_birthdate_at_epoch_minimum_boundary
preconditions: Clinician user exists with role=clinician
Patient resource exists with birthDate set to '1900-01-01' (minimum representable FHIR date) and is assigned to the clinician
Valid bearer token obtained for the clinician
actions: Inspect the birthDate field in the response
expected: No date parsing or serialization error in the response
trace: R-HLT-027 boundary case: minimum valid birthDate timestamp
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_patient_birthdate_at_epoch_minimum_boundary(
    api_client,
    clinician_token,
    boundary_dob_min_patient_id,
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{boundary_dob_min_patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("birthDate") == "1900-01-01"

def test_patient_birthdate_at_epoch_maximum_boundary(

tests.name: test_audit_log_written_after_successful_patient_read
preconditions: Clinician user exists with role=clinician
Patient resource exists and is assigned to the clinician
Audit log is empty or baseline count is recorded for the patient/clinician pair
Valid bearer token obtained for the clinician
actions: Record baseline audit entry count via <get_audit_count> helper
Retrieve audit entries via <get_audit_entries> helper filtered by patient and clinician
expected: HTTP 200 OK on the patient read
Audit entry count increases by exactly 1
Audit entry action is 'read' and references the correct patient id and clinician id
trace: R-HLT-027 expected outcome: access audit written after successful read
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_audit_log_written_after_successful_patient_read(
    api_client,
    clinician_token,
    assigned_patient_id,
    clinician_id,
    get_audit_count,
    get_latest_audit_entry,
):
    baseline = get_audit_count(patient_id=assigned_patient_id, clinician_id=clinician_id)
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{assigned_patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    new_count = get_audit_count(patient_id=assigned_patient_id, clinician_id=clinician_id)
    assert new_count == baseline + 1
    entry = get_latest_audit_entry(patient_id=assigned_patient_id, clinician_id=clinician_id)
    assert entry["action"] == "read"
    assert str(entry["patientId"]) == str(assigned_patient_id)
    assert str(entry["clinicianId"]) == str(clinician_id)

tests.name: test_auth_decision_table_role_x_assignment_combinations
preconditions: Clinician token and non-clinician (admin) token are available
Two patients exist: one assigned to clinician, one not assigned
Four combinations: (clinician, assigned), (clinician, unassigned), (admin, assigned), (admin, unassigned)
actions: Parameterize over (role, patient_id, expected_status) tuples covering all four decision table rows
GET /Patient/{patient_id} with the appropriate token for each row
expected: (admin, assigned) -> 403 (admin role not permitted on this endpoint)
trace: R-HLT-027 decision table: role and assignment determine access
tests.executable:
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "token_fixture, patient_fixture, expected_status",
    [
        ("clinician_token", "assigned_patient_id", 200),
        ("clinician_token", "unassigned_patient_id", 403),
        ("admin_token", "assigned_patient_id", 403),
        ("admin_token", "unassigned_patient_id", 403),
    ],
)
def test_auth_decision_table_role_x_assignment_combinations(
    request,
    api_client,
    token_fixture,
    patient_fixture,
    expected_status,
):
    token = request.getfixturevalue(token_fixture)
    patient_id = request.getfixturevalue(patient_fixture)
    resp = api_client.get(
        f"/Patient/{patient_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == expected_status
